content.subtitle: Form
items: subject + have in the correct form + object + past participle

content.subtitle: Use
rows: To indicate that a subject arranges for somebody else to perform a service or task.
The manager **has** her office **redecorated** every year.

content.subtitle: Tense / modal
rows: The manager **has** her office **redecorated** every year.
She **is having** the walls **painted** at the moment.
She **was having** the computers **repaired** when I visited.
She **will have** a new sofa **delivered**. / She **would have** new curtains **put up**.
She **is going to have** a new printer **installed**.
She might stop **having** the office **cleaned** so often.

content.subtitle: Active vs causative
items: Normal active sentence: A decorator paints the office every year.
Causative sentence: The manager **has** the office **painted** every year.

content.subtitle: Have vs get
items: Get can be used instead of have. Get is more informal than have.
I'm going to **have** my laptop repaired. (more formal)
I'm going to **get** my laptop repaired. (more informal)

content.subtitle: By + agent
items: As with the passive, we can use by to show who does the action.
We're having our wedding cake made **by** a local bakery.

content.subtitle: Important rules
items: In the causative, have always comes before the noun and the past participle comes after the noun.
In questions, the past participle stays after the noun: **Did** you **have** the windows **cleaned** yesterday?
Some verbs have irregular past participle forms.

sections.title: A: Complete the sentences
sections.instructions: Use the causative form of have and the verb in brackets.

items.sentence: He ...... his suit ...... for the ceremony tomorrow. (clean)
We ...... our air conditioner ...... before summer. (service)
I ...... my phone screen ...... before I sold it. (replace)
A plumber repaired our shower yesterday.
items.answer: We had our shower repaired yesterday.

items.sentence: A stylist is colouring Nina's hair now.
items.answer: Nina is having her hair coloured now.

items.sentence: A company will deliver their new sofa tomorrow.
items.answer: They will have their new sofa delivered tomorrow.

items.sentence: A technician is updating his computer software.
items.answer: He is having his computer software updated.

items.sentence: A gardener trims their trees every spring.
items.answer: They have their trees trimmed every spring.

items.sentence: An optician tested my eyes last week.
items.answer: I had my eyes tested last week.

sections.title: C: Rewrite using the causative
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the causative. Use no more than four words.

items.context: A mechanic fixed her bike yesterday.
Someone is going to decorate their flat next month.
items.sentence: They are ...... their flat decorated next month.

items.context: A dentist cleaned his teeth last Friday.
items.sentence: He ...... his teeth cleaned last Friday.

items.context: Someone washes their car every week.
items.sentence: They ...... their car washed every week.

items.context: A photographer took her passport photo yesterday.
items.sentence: She ...... her passport photo taken yesterday.

items.context: A designer is making their company logo.
items.sentence: They are ...... their company logo made.
